# Deep Dive: Ads, Leads, and Attribution

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

## Core idea

Advertising systems convert measurements into money. Fraud or low-quality traffic exploits the gap between what dashboards count and what businesses actually need.

## Three failure modes

| Failure mode | Description | Harm |
|---|---|---|
| Fake traffic | Clicks/impressions not from genuine interest | Wasted spend, polluted optimization |
| Fake leads | Forms filled by bots, farms, or low-intent sources | Sales-team waste, false CPL success |
| Attribution theft | Real conversion credited to wrong source | Budget misallocation, dishonest commissions |

## Why dashboards are vulnerable

- Metrics are easy to count.
- Quality is slower to verify.
- Vendors may be rewarded for volume.
- Algorithms optimize toward submitted conversion events.
- Sales outcomes lag behind ad reporting.

## Audit questions

- Are leads contactable?
- Do they match service geography?
- Are form responses repetitive?
- Does channel-reported conversion match CRM reality?
- Do installs produce retention or in-app behaviour?
- Are suspicious sources being rewarded because they are cheap?
